run_summary: One UNDERDONE flag at confidence 3 (GTLB) clears all gates cleanly: earnings in 4 trading days, no prior position, thesis grounded in specific filings mechanics (mid-quarter Reg FD reaffirmation + RIF + buyback + cash flow inflection + enterprise ARR growth). Screen 2's conf-3 UNDERDONE track record is 50% (1 hit, 1 miss in a 2-call sample), below the 57% overall average but not disqualifying. The flag's pedagogical depth is strong — specific filing reads, named what-confirms and what-kills, learning thesis. BUY.

entries.name: GitLab
entries.thesis: The May 11 pre-announcement is a deliberate de-risking move by management — they would not have reaffirmed guidance and announced a RIF (typically a negative-perception event) 3 weeks before print unless Q1 was tracking at or above plan. Combined with the buyback authorization and the FY26 cash flow inflection (-8% to +24% OCF margin) plus 26% growth in $1M+ ARR cohort, the filings posture is meaningfully more constructive than a stock that has had multiple CFO transitions (Robins out Sept 2025, Shen interim, Ross arriving Jan 2026) and a securities class action overhang would typically suggest.
entries.what_kills: NDR drops further below 118% reported in FY26 (already down from 123% prior year); large enterprise deals slip; Duo Agent Platform (GA Jan 2026) shows weak early monetization data; RIF framed defensively rather than reinvestment-driven on the call.
entries.reasoning: Re-entry guard: GTLB was closed 2d ago for a gain (realized 1.73%). Re-opening a recently-closed name requires flag confidence >= 4; this flag is confidence 3. Downgraded to WATCH. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: Classification UNDERDONE, confidence 3 (meets min_buy_confidence threshold). Earnings date 2026-06-02 is 4 trading days out and has not yet passed (gate: currency ✓). Thesis grounded in specific filings mechanics: May 11 Reg FD 8-K reaffirming Q1 FY27 guidance mid-quarter + announcement of RIF with reinvestment narrative + $400M buyback authorization + 10-K showing FY26 OCF margin swing from -8% to +24% and 26% growth in $1M+ ARR cohort. The thesis rests on the specific filing tell that management would not reaffirm guidance 3 weeks pre-print unless tracking at or above plan. What-confirms is granular (NDR, cohort growth, margin benefit, FY27 guide maintenance/raise). What-kills is named (NDR deterioration, deal slips, Duo Agent monetization miss, defensive RIF framing). Pedagogical depth is strong. No existing position in GTLB in portfolio (gate: no duplicate ✓). Cash position $10,058 on $10k bankroll provides clear headroom for a 15–25% conviction-sized entry (gate: cash ✓). SaaS sector currently unweighted in open positions; no sector constraint (gate: sector ✓). All gates passed.)

red_team.weakest_link: The thesis assumes management's mid-quarter Reg FD reaffirmation is a de-risking signal of confidence, but reaffirmations three weeks pre-print are routine housekeeping that do not necessarily imply beats or raises.
red_team.critique: The core tell—that management would not reaffirm guidance unless tracking at or above plan—is unsupported. Reg FD reaffirmations three weeks before earnings are standard practice and carry minimal information content; they often serve compliance and risk-mitigation purposes rather than signaling hidden strength. The RIF announcement alongside reaffirmation could equally signal management is bracing for margin pressure or growth deceleration and is pre-spinning the narrative defensively. The 10-K shows improvement in OCF margin swing and $1M+ ARR cohort growth, but those are past-tense metrics from FY26; forward guidance maintenance on May 11 does not confirm those trends persist into Q1 FY27. NDR already fell from 123% to 118% year-over-year, showing deceleration momentum that a reaffirmation does not resolve.
